compiled_state.title: As the DSO merge workflow, merge-to-main.sh automatically processes PR comments after CI passes and before merge
compiled_state.author: Test
compiled_state.description:
## Description

**What**: A new comment_response phase added to merge-to-main.sh that runs after the CI phase completes (CI has passed) and before the merge phase, processing all unresolved PR comments via the shared pr-comment-response.sh pipeline.
**Why**: Automated merge workflows need to address PR review comments before merging. Without this phase, engineer comments on PRs go unaddressed when using merge-to-main.sh.
**Scope**:
- IN: New comment_response phase entry in _ALL_PHASES array positioned after ci_trigger and before merge; phase captures a fixed snapshot of comments at phase entry (no new comments arriving mid-phase affect the current run); when accept-classified comments produce new commits, the CI polling loop re-runs against the updated HEAD SHA (reusing the existing gh pr checks loop mechanism from the ci_trigger phase) before merge proceeds; --resume compatibility: resuming from a state file that predates the comment_response phase addition correctly positions execution (does not skip the phase, does not error out, correctly determines whether the phase has run); phase follows the existing merge-to-main.sh phase template pattern (state file update, phase-level error handling)
- OUT: Standalone invocation (Story 5); comment classification logic (delegated to Story 5's skill and pr-comment-response.sh); changes to any existing phase's behavior

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, merge-to-main.sh _ALL_PHASES array includes comment_response positioned after ci_trigger and before merge
  ← Satisfies: "merge-to-main.sh gains a comment_response phase inserted after CI passes and before the merge phase" (SC5)
- When this story is complete, when accept-classified comments produce new commits, the CI polling loop re-runs against the updated HEAD SHA before the merge phase executes
  ← Satisfies: "when accept-classified comments produce new commits, CI polling re-runs via the existing gh pr checks loop against the updated HEAD SHA before merge" (SC5)
- When this story is complete, running merge-to-main.sh --resume from a state file created before the comment_response phase was added correctly handles the missing phase state without error or silent skip
  ← Satisfies: "--resume correctly positions the phase in the existing sequence, including correct behavior when an existing state file predates the phase addition" (SC5)
- When this story is complete, unit tests are written and passing for all new or modified logic
  ← TDD Done-of-Done requirement

## Considerations

- [Reliability] Phase ordering: comment_response must run after CI has fully completed and passed — verify that the ci_trigger phase in merge-to-main.sh includes CI polling to completion (not just firing CI asynchronously); document this assumption
- [Reliability] State file schema must be extended to track comment_response phase completion; --resume from state files both with and without the new phase key must work correctly
- [Reliability] Phase must not allow merge to proceed if the CI re-poll (after accept commits) fails — comment_response + re-poll must fully complete successfully before merge
- [Performance] comment_response phase adds API calls (fetch) and potentially a full CI wait (after accept commits) to merge-to-main.sh runtime — document the expected time impact in the phase
- [Maintainability] comment_response phase addition requires CLAUDE.md Architecture section update (merge-to-main phases list) — handled by Story D

## Escalation Policy

**Escalation policy**: Proceed unless a significant assumption is required to continue — one that could send the implementation in the wrong direction. Escalate only when genuinely blocked without a reasonable inference. Document all assumptions made without escalating.
